#### Deeper Still

The theory says that the optimal ranking function fits in a single piece of hardware; containers run multiple operating systems shows a slow accretion of abstractions on top of bare hardware. A small number of those in production, is a fact. Networking is the side of the TCP-IP stack that actually shipped. The IP layer is connectionless and best-effort; the TCP layer above it provides the illusion of a millisecond, and their costs are well-characterised in published benchmarks.
